compiled_state.title: EAGAIN-aware retry and reclassification in test runner infrastructure
compiled_state.author: Main
compiled_state.description:
## Context
Developers running the DSO test suite locally encounter OS-level resource exhaustion (EAGAIN/errno 35) when parallel test execution saturates process limits. These failures manifest as "Resource temporarily unavailable" in stderr and get recorded as test logic failures, blocking commits via the test gate even though the tests themselves are correct. Today the only workaround is manually reducing MAX_PARALLEL or re-running.

## Success Criteria
1. Suite-engine's _process_completed_test function detects EAGAIN patterns (fork: (retry: )?Resource temporarily unavailable, BlockingIOError.*Resource temporarily unavailable) in the test's captured stderr after non-zero exit, and re-invokes that single test file with MAX_PARALLEL=1 exported as an environment variable — subject to the existing TEST_TIMEOUT ceiling
2. Record-test-status.sh applies the same EAGAIN grep pattern as a safety net, writing resource_exhaustion status (line 1 of test-gate-status) instead of failed when the pattern matches — only when suite-engine has not already retried and written passed
3. The test gate (pre-commit-test-gate.sh) treats resource_exhaustion status as non-blocking at both check points (fast-path and full-path)
4. Resource exhaustion events are visible in test-gate-status output as a distinct resource_exhaustion status value, and all existing parsers of test-gate-status (validate.sh, pre-commit-test-gate.sh, record-test-status.sh) handle the new value without error
5. Exit code 254 (macOS fork failure) combined with EAGAIN stderr pattern triggers retry/reclassification — both conditions must match (exit code alone is insufficient)
6. When suite-engine retries a test and the retry passes, the retry result (passed) is the authoritative status — the original EAGAIN stderr is not re-evaluated by record-test-status.sh
7. An integration test deliberately induces EAGAIN (via ulimit -u to constrain process count) and verifies the full retry to reclassification to non-blocking gate path end-to-end

## Scenario Analysis
- EAGAIN pattern match fires on test output that embeds the string (high): Tests that deliberately print the error string to stderr could be misclassified. Mitigation: dual-condition gate limits false positives.
- Race between suite-engine retry and record-test-status.sh (medium): Addressed by SC6.
- MAX_PARALLEL=1 not exported (high): Addressed by SC1.
- record-test-status.sh overwrites suite-engine's passed (medium): Addressed by SC6.
- Downstream parsers expect only passed/failed (medium): Addressed by SC4.

## Dependencies
- Cross-runtime harness adapter epic (2239-1545): no blocking dependency, coordinate if both in-flight.

## Approach
Pattern-match-and-retry at both layers. Suite-engine detects EAGAIN in _process_completed_test and retries with MAX_PARALLEL=1 exported. Record-test-status.sh applies the same grep as safety net, deferring to suite-engine's retry result. Test gate treats resource_exhaustion as non-blocking but visible.
